cases.rationale: Family, prayer, and holiday practices are repeatedly shown; no direct action against Tradition appears.
Work and family decisions show no direct choice clearly contrary to Security; worries alone are not treated as negative behavior.
The displayed work, task-force, and hobby actions do not directly oppose Achievement; frustration is not labeled behavior.
Cautious work and family choices are consistent with or neutral to Security, with no direct contrary choice shown.
Furniture restoration and workshop planning are competence-directed, with no direct action against Achievement.
Teaching and water-advocacy actions pursue broad welfare; no direct contrary behavior to Universalism appears.
Leadership and career actions are neutral to or support Power; exclusion and feelings are not the writer's contrary actions.
At position 1 the writer stays silent while another presents their idea; later independent project choices are not negative.
Protocol-following and family/social restraint are consistent with Conformity; no contrary action appears.
Job change, technical learning, and novel activities do not directly conflict with Stimulation.
At 1 the writer defers despite their proposal; at 7 they copy Diego's style to seek acceptance. The yes entries are separated.
At 2 the writer spends twelve painful library hours; the other displayed entries contain pleasure or neutral work.
At 4 and 5 the writer drinks secretly against maternal norms and has stopped regular prayer, an adjacent conflict run; later tradition-affirming actions recover it, though a later lie is isolated.
Cautious, polite, and rule-aware actions are consistent with Conformity.
Translation and garden actions aim at other people and environmental welfare; no contrary behavior appears.
Creative teaching, camping, and considering an innovation lab do not oppose Stimulation; imposed routine is not a negative choice.
At 2 and 6 the writer lets others override them rather than asserting authority; those choices are not adjacent.
Rest, cabin time, work boundaries, and cooking are pleasure-oriented rather than against Hedonism.
At 2 the writer chooses caregiving over planned personal quiet; surrounding actions include pleasures and neutral obligations, with no adjacent yes.
At 1 the writer stays extra and cancels a planned dinner; the rota disruption is externally imposed and the later swap protects Goa.
At 1, 3, 6, 7, and 11 the writer accepts overriding or permission limits; 6 and 7 are adjacent, followed by renewed direct leadership.
At 1 the writer avoids challenging anti-refugee remarks and at 2 orders potentially polluting components; later donation and sourcing actions reverse that run.
The unstable rota and double shifts are externally imposed; the writer seeks predictable scheduling rather than choosing against Security.
At 2, 6, and 9 concealment and relationship decisions depart from the religious family script; they are separated by tradition-affirming behavior and final honesty.
At 1 the writer leaves household needs waiting; at 8 and 9 they decline action supporting overburdened peers, an adjacent current run.
At 1 the writer leaves family waiting after repeated promises; later restraint and relationship-preserving choices are not contrary to Conformity.
Travel, dancing, exploration, and project novelty pursue Stimulation rather than oppose it.
Improving students, preparing materials and data, and recognition are not direct contrary Achievement actions.
No displayed behavior is clearly contrary to Achievement; job-change tradeoffs are ambiguous rather than labeled negative.
At 1 the writer yields on a substantive routing proposal; the subsequent pilot and presentation are achievement-directed.
Restoration and workshop actions are neutral to or create fresh challenges, with no contrary Stimulation act.
At 3 and 5 the writer stays silent or cedes strategic control; the entries are not adjacent.
At 1 and 4 the writer lets gig work displace family Sunday and Koningsdag rituals; later visits, grace, and church reaffirm Tradition.
The writer's interest in and booking of Nairobi travel pursue novelty, not a Stimulation conflict.
At 3 the writer snaps at their vulnerable mother; surrounding care and boundary actions otherwise do not oppose Benevolence.
Family rootedness, saving, and declining travel nursing support or are neutral to Security.
Caregiving and limited shared responsibility uphold close others' welfare; the writer calls back and shares planning, with no contrary Benevolence choice.
Translations, accessible recycling, advocacy, and cleanup support broad welfare; no direct contrary action appears.
At 3 and 5 care and an extra shift cost the writer rest or planned pleasure; they are separated by neutral/self-care action.
Career effort, visibility, and promotion behavior support or are neutral to Achievement.
Conflict avoidance, agreement at playgroup, and accepting Sunday dinner are Conformity-aligned.
At 2 the writer rejects an expected placement for their own project and at 3 refuses help to keep it entirely theirs, an adjacent active conflict.
